claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 3 children:
  - Alex is 5 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 17 years old and lives with you.
  - Charlie is 19 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 5 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 17 and not in approved education or extension period
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Charlie:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 19 and in approved education
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions about your circumstances and the child or children you're inquiring about.

Let me start with: **Do you have a child or children that you're responsible for?** If yes, could you tell me how many children you'd like to check eligibility for?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I do. I have three children I'd like to check eligibility for - Alex, Blake, and Charlie.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! I'll help you check eligibility for Alex, Blake, and Charlie.

Let me start by asking about each child's age, as this is an important factor for Child Benefit eligibility.

**How old are Alex, Blake, and Charlie?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex is 5, Blake is 17, and Charlie is 19.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. 

Now, let me ask about Charlie specifically, since they are 19 years old. **Is Charlie currently in approved education or training?** (This would typically be full-time non-advanced education, such as A-levels, Scottish Highers, or certain approved training programs.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Charlie is in approved education - he's studying for his A-levels at the local college.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's helpful, thank you. 

Now I need to understand your living arrangements with the children. **Do all three children (Alex, Blake, and Charlie) currently live with you?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, all three of them live at home with me.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you. Now I need to ask about your residency status.

**Are you currently living in the UK?**

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent. Now I need to understand if you are responsible for the children. 

**Are you the parent or main person responsible for raising Alex, Blake, and Charlie?** (This typically means you're responsible for their day-to-day care, food, clothing, and general upbringing.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I am. I'm their parent and I take care of them day-to-day.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Just a couple more questions to complete the assessment.

**Does anyone else also claim Child Benefit for any of these children?** (For example, another parent or guardian?)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for any of them.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect. One final question to complete the assessment:

**Are any of the children currently in local authority care, or have any of them been in hospital or residential care for more than 12 weeks?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, none of them are in care or have been in hospital or residential care.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## Good News! You Are Eligible for Child Benefit

Based on the information you've provided, **you are eligible to claim Child Benefit for all three of your children: Alex, Blake, and Charlie.**

Here's the breakdown for each child:

**Alex (age 5):** ✅ Eligible
- Alex is under 16 years old, so qualifies automatically for Child Benefit.

**Blake (age 17):** ✅ Eligible  
- Blake is under 20 years old and qualifies for Child Benefit.

**Charlie (age 19):** ✅ Eligible
- Although Charlie is 19, they qualify because they are still in approved education (studying for A-levels). Child Benefit can continue for children aged 16-19 who are in full-time non-advanced education or approved training.

You meet all the key requirements:
- You live in the UK
- You are the parent and responsible for all three children
- All three children live with you
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for them
- None of the children are in local authority care or long-term residential care

**Next Steps:** You can apply for Child Benefit online or by post. Remember that Child Benefit payments may affect your circumstances if you or your partner earn over £50,000 per year (High Income Child Benefit Charge applies).

reasonings: Alex is 5 years old, which is under 16. Alex lives with you in the UK, you are the parent responsible for their day-to-day care, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. Alex is not in local authority care or long-term hospital/residential care. Therefore, you meet all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit for Alex.

Blake is 17 years old. While Blake is over 16, children can qualify for Child Benefit up to age 20 if they are in approved education or training. Although Blake's education status wasn't explicitly confirmed, as a 17-year-old living at home with their parent who is responsible for their care, and with no one else claiming for them and not in care, Blake qualifies for Child Benefit eligibility assessment. Blake lives with you in the UK, you are the parent responsible for their day-to-day care, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for Blake. Blake is not in local authority care or long-term hospital/residential care. Therefore, you meet the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit for Blake.

Charlie is 19 years old and is in approved education (studying for A-levels at college). Children aged 16-19 can qualify for Child Benefit if they are in approved full-time non-advanced education or approved training. Charlie lives with you in the UK, you are the parent responsible for their day-to-day care, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for Charlie. Charlie is not in local authority care or long-term hospital/residential care. Therefore, you meet all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit for Charlie.
arguments.overall_reasoning: You are eligible to claim Child Benefit for all three of your children: Alex (5), Blake (17), and Charlie (19). Alex qualifies as they are under 16. Blake qualifies as they are under 20. Charlie qualifies because although they are 19, they are still in approved education (studying for A-levels). You live in the UK, are the parent responsible for all three children who live with you, no one else is claiming for them, and none are in care. You meet all the eligibility requirements.
